brands.name: Goin' Postal
brands.notes: Independent retail shipping/pack-and-ship franchise (GP Brands, Inc., Florida) carrier-neutral across USPS, UPS, FedEx, and DHL. Actively recruiting (FDD effective 8/15/2025) with a low-cost, flat-fee royalty model rather than a percentage of sales. Roughly 170 units; franchisor does not publish a state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Very low average revenue / small-box economics with escalating flat royalty
riskFlags.detail: Goin' Postal runs a small-footprint, owner-operated retail shipping model competing directly with The UPS Store, PostalAnnex, and big-box carriers. The flat monthly royalty ($460-$550/mo for new 2025 franchisees) escalates roughly 5% every third year for the full 15-year term regardless of store sales, a fixed obligation that pressures low-volume stores.
riskFlags.source: Goin' Postal 2025 FDD (service.goinpostal.com); Vetted Biz

riskFlags.title: Small, slow-growth system in a category dominated by larger brands
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur lists roughly 170 units with a 20-person headquarters; the brand is dwarfed by The UPS Store (5,000+) and competes on lower entry cost rather than scale or brand recognition, limiting national marketing leverage for franchisees.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory

riskFlags.title: No litigation or bankruptcy disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services report Goin' Postal does not disclose lawsuits or bankruptcy information in its FDD - a clean Item 3/Item 4 record, though the system is small.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz / FranchiseOverview FDD analyses

liquidCapitalMin.note: Cash requirement cited as $20,000; Vetted Biz cites a $10,000 minimum cash figure.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Franchise Help directory
totalInvestmentMin.note: FDD-derived Item 7 range $46,750-$109,500 (Entrepreneur); some directories cite up to $128,500-$139,500.

franchiseFee.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Goin' Postal 2025 FDD
franchiseFee.note: Up to $15,000; some FDD-derived sources show a lower-tier fee around $9,950.

yearFounded.note: Some directories cite 2004 founding; Entrepreneur shows 2002 founded, 2004 franchising.

veteranDiscount.note: 50% off the franchise fee for veterans.

financingOffered.note: Third-party financing available; no direct franchisor financing indicated.

thirdPartyFinancing.value: Third-party financing available per Entrepreneur directory

businessModelType.source: Goin' Postal franchisor site (retail and business shipping services)

mobileBased.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory (retail storefront; home/mobile operation not allowed)

fddAvailable.source: Goin' Postal 2025 FDD (publicly posted at service.goinpostal.com)

brands.name: Launch Trampoline Park
brands.notes: Family entertainment center / indoor trampoline-park franchise (Launch Family Entertainment, Warwick, RI), franchising since 2013. A capital-intensive big-box leisure investment ($1.9M-$4.5M) with ~25 locations. Registered in 13 franchise-registration states; no public all-states availability statement, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Multimillion-dollar build cost with mandatory minimum local ad spend
riskFlags.detail: 2026 FDD Item 7 runs roughly $1,899,900-$4,524,500 including a $75,000 franchise fee, and ongoing fees stack a 6% royalty, a 1% brand development fund, and local advertising of the greater of 4% of sales or $4,000/month - a heavy fixed-cost base for a discretionary-spend leisure category.
riskFlags.source: FranchiseOverview FDD summary; SHARPSHEETS Launch Trampoline Park FDD analysis

riskFlags.title: Discretionary, injury-exposed leisure category with thin sample disclosure
riskFlags.detail: Trampoline parks face elevated liability/insurance exposure and demand that contracts in recessions; the Item 19 financial performance representation is based on a small 6-unit sample (average ~$2.0M, range $966K-$3.5M), giving limited visibility into systemwide economics on an ~25-unit base.
riskFlags.source: FranchiseOverview FDD summary (Item 19); SHARPSHEETS

riskFlags.title: Small system with a meaningful company-owned share
riskFlags.detail: The system totals about 25 units (21 franchised, 4 company-owned), so a single franchisee carries significant concentration risk and the brand has limited national marketing scale relative to larger FEC competitors like Sky Zone and Urban Air.
riskFlags.source: FranchiseOverview FDD summary

totalInvestmentMin.note: FDD Item 7 range $1,899,900-$4,524,500 including the $75,000 franchise fee. Some directories cite higher full-build ranges up to ~$6.3M-$6.5M depending on facility size.

adFundPercent.note: 1% brand development fund, plus mandatory local advertising of the greater of 4% of sales or $4,000/month.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Average and median ~$2.0M across a 6-unit reporting sample; range $966,000-$3,500,000.

yearFounded.source: SHARPSHEETS Launch Trampoline Park FDD analysis
yearFounded.note: First park opened in Warwick, RI; franchising began 2013.

trainingWeeks.note: Approximately four weeks (55 hours classroom, 105 hours on-the-job) in Warwick, RI or a franchisor-designated site.

isGrowing.note: Unit trend not clearly disclosed; system remains small at ~25 units.

mobileBased.source: Launch franchisor site (large indoor FEC facility model)

brands.name: Pinot's Palette
brands.notes: Paint-and-sip studio franchise (founded 2009/2010, Houston, TX), acquired by Twist Brands LLC (parent of Painting with a Twist) in September 2024. ~67 US studios, all franchised. The franchisor markets renewed post-acquisition growth, but the longer-run picture is a sharply contracted system - buyers should weigh peak-to-now unit loss against the current expansion pitch.

riskFlags.title: System contracted more than 50% from its peak
riskFlags.detail: Pinot's Palette had 140+ studios across 31 states by January 2018; current FDD-derived data shows roughly 67 US studios - more than a 50% reduction - reflecting maturation and shakeout in the discretionary paint-and-sip category.
riskFlags.source: Franchising.com / Wikipedia (2018 peak ~140+ studios); Vetted Biz (67, 2025)

riskFlags.title: Low average unit revenue and long payback
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz Item 19 analysis cites average yearly gross sales of about $379,708 with estimated owner-operator earnings of roughly $53,000-$68,000 and a payback period of 3.6-5.6 years - thin economics for a labor- and event-driven discretionary model.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Pinot's Palette Franchise Insights (Item 19-derived)

riskFlags.title: Brand now one of several under Twist Brands platform
riskFlags.detail: Following the September 2024 acquisition, Pinot's Palette sits alongside Painting with a Twist (a much larger paint-and-sip brand) inside Twist Brands; the franchisor controls development priorities and shared services across competing sister concepts.
riskFlags.source: International Franchise Association (franchise.org) acquisition coverage

liquidCapitalMin.source: Vetted Biz - Pinot's Palette Franchise Insights

totalInvestmentMin.source: Vetted Biz - Pinot's Palette Franchise Insights

totalInvestmentMax.source: Vetted Biz - Pinot's Palette Franchise Insights

royaltyPercent.note: 6% of monthly sales per Vetted Biz; some directories cite 7%.

trainingWeeks.source: Vetted Biz - Pinot's Palette Franchise Insights
trainingWeeks.note: Approximately two weeks at corporate (Mandeville, LA / Houston).

isGrowing.note: Long-run contraction, though franchisor markets post-acquisition expansion in CA, NV, FL, and OH for 2025.

brands.name: Board & Brush Creative Studio
brands.notes: DIY wood-sign workshop franchise (Hartland, WI), founded 2016 and franchising since 2017. Low entry cost (~$75K-$100K) but a contracting system with notably low average unit revenue. Franchisor does not publish a state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: System contracting with a high 2024 closure rate
riskFlags.detail: Franchised units fell from 261 in 2022 to roughly 194 in 2024, with an estimated 21% unit closure rate in 2024 - a meaningful shakeout in a discretionary workshop-events category.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz / FranchiseLens Board & Brush analyses (FDD outlet data)

riskFlags.title: Very low average unit revenue
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz Item 19 analysis cites average yearly gross sales of roughly $110,000-$116,000 with estimated owner-operator earnings of about $13,000-$17,000 and a 6.9-8.9 year payback - thin economics highly dependent on event volume.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Board & Brush Franchise Insights (Item 19-derived)

riskFlags.title: Minimum monthly royalty regardless of revenue
riskFlags.detail: The 6% royalty carries a minimum payment of $400/month, a fixed obligation that weighs disproportionately on low-volume studios given the system's modest average sales.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS / Vetted Biz Board & Brush FDD analyses

liquidCapitalMin.source: Vetted Biz - Board & Brush Franchise Insights
liquidCapitalMin.note: Minimum cash required $15,000; some directories cite up to $75,000 liquid and $300,000 net worth.

brands.name: AR Workshop
brands.notes: DIY home-decor workshop franchise (AR Workshop Franchising, LLC, Fort Mill, SC), founded 2016 and franchising since 2016/2017. Hands-on wood-sign, canvas, and home-decor crafting classes plus DIY-to-go kits. ~115 US studios; franchisor does not publish a state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Litigation and/or bankruptcy disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services report that AR Workshop discloses lawsuit and/or bankruptcy information in its Franchise Disclosure Document - unlike most peers in the DIY-workshop category, which report clean Item 3/Item 4 records. Prospective buyers should review the current FDD Item 3 and Item 4 directly.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - AR Workshop Franchise Insights (FDD analysis)

riskFlags.title: Very low average unit revenue and long payback
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz Item 19 analysis cites average yearly gross sales of roughly $119,841 with estimated owner-operator earnings of about $16,800-$21,600 and a 10-12 year payback period - thin economics for a discretionary, event-driven workshop concept.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - AR Workshop Franchise Insights (Item 19-derived)

riskFlags.title: Direct competition with similar DIY-workshop concepts in a contracting category
riskFlags.detail: AR Workshop competes head-to-head with Board & Brush, Pinot's Palette, and other DIY/paint-and-sip studios; several peers have contracted in recent years, signaling category maturity and shakeout risk for new single-studio operators.
riskFlags.source: FranchiseGrade / Vetted Biz AR Workshop reviews

brands.name: Color Me Mine
brands.notes: Paint-your-own-pottery studio franchise (Color Me Mine Enterprises), founded 1991 with the modern franchise program from 2014; ~113 US studios (nearly all franchised). Higher average unit revenue than most DIY-workshop peers. Franchisor does not publish a state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: High build-out cost for a discretionary studio concept
riskFlags.detail: FDD Item 7 runs $180,000-$437,700 for a paint-your-own-pottery studio - a substantial fit-out for a discretionary-spend retail experience, with a 6.9-8.9 year estimated payback per Vetted Biz analysis.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS / Vetted Biz Color Me Mine FDD analyses

riskFlags.title: Stacked marketing obligations plus monthly service fee
riskFlags.detail: Beyond the 5% royalty, franchisees pay a 1% national marketing fund, are required to spend 2% of sales on local advertising, and pay an information/accounting service fee of $80/month - fixed obligations independent of revenue.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Help / Franchise Clique Color Me Mine summaries

riskFlags.title: Mature concept in a competitive DIY-experience category
riskFlags.detail: Color Me Mine has operated since 1991 and competes in the broad paint-your-own-pottery / DIY-experience space alongside paint-and-sip and craft-workshop concepts; the system has held roughly flat in the low-100s of units rather than expanding rapidly.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Vetted Biz

liquidCapitalMin.source: Vetted Biz - Color Me Mine Franchise Insights

brands.name: Office Evolution
brands.notes: Suburban coworking / flexible-office and virtual-office franchise (founded 2003 in Boulder, CO; franchising since 2012), now part of United Franchise Group's UFG Coworks division alongside Venture X. ~84 franchised centers, all franchised (company exited direct ownership in 2024). Franchisor does not publish a state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Long-term commercial lease exposure in a structurally shifting office market
riskFlags.detail: The coworking model carries multi-year commercial real-estate leases against month-to-month or short-term member revenue - a duration mismatch that magnifies downside in a post-pandemic office market still adjusting to hybrid/remote work. Build-out can reach ~$2.2M for larger centers.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter 2025 FDD review (Item 7 range $193,000-$2,178,000)

riskFlags.title: High minimum royalty and marketing fees regardless of revenue
riskFlags.detail: Royalty is the greater of 7.5% of gross revenues or $1,500/month, and the marketing fee is the greater of 3% of gross revenues or $1,500/month - combined fixed minimums of $3,000/month that weigh heavily on ramping or under-occupied centers.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter 2025 FDD review

riskFlags.title: Slow net unit growth on a small base
riskFlags.detail: Franchised outlets were essentially flat in 2023 (78 to 78) and grew modestly to 84 in 2024; the system remains small (~84 centers) relative to coworking competitors, limiting national brand leverage even as systemwide revenue rose to $46.3M in 2024.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter 2025 FDD review (outlet tables)

royaltyPercent.note: Greater of 7.5% of gross revenues or $1,500/month.

adFundPercent.note: Greater of 3% of gross revenues or $1,500/month.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Office Evolution 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2024 average gross sales across 75 qualifying locations; centers over 10,000 sq ft averaged $733,050, under 10,000 sq ft $504,308; 92% average peak occupancy.

yearFounded.source: Office Evolution corporate blog (founded 2003 in Boulder, CO)

yearFranchising.source: Office Evolution corporate site; United Franchise Group

isGrowing.note: +6 net franchised units in 2024 after a flat 2023.

businessModelType.source: Office Evolution franchisor site (coworking/flexible office for small businesses)

mobileBased.source: Office Evolution franchisor site (physical business-center model)

brands.name: National Property Inspections
brands.notes: Home and commercial property inspection franchise (NPI, Omaha, NE), franchising since 1987 - one of the oldest inspection franchises. Low-cost, home/mobile-based owner-operator model (~194 units). Franchisor does not publish a state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Low average revenue with high royalty plus minimum royalty floor
riskFlags.detail: Item 19-derived data shows average gross sales of about $122,339 (median $86,016), against an 8% royalty plus a 2% advertising fee and monthly royalty minimums escalating from $300 to $500 - a high effective fee burden on a low-revenue, single-operator model.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS NPI FDD analyses

riskFlags.title: System contracting and tied to housing-transaction volume
riskFlags.detail: Units declined from roughly 214 in 2022 to about 194 in 2024. Inspection demand is highly cyclical with home sales, so franchisee revenue is exposed to interest-rate-driven swings in real-estate transaction volume.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS NPI FDD analysis (outlet trend)

riskFlags.title: No litigation or bankruptcy disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services report NPI does not disclose lawsuits or bankruptcy information in its FDD - a clean Item 3/Item 4 record for a long-established system.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - NPI Franchise Insights (FDD analysis)

liquidCapitalMin.note: Minimum cash required $10,000; other directories cite ~$34,900 liquid.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Vetted Biz - NPI Franchise Insights; SHARPSHEETS

totalInvestmentMax.source: Vetted Biz - NPI Franchise Insights; SHARPSHEETS

royaltyPercent.note: 8% of monthly gross sales with monthly minimums escalating from $300 to $500.

avgUnitRevenue.source: NPI FDD Item 19 (via SHARPSHEETS and Vetted Biz)

totalUnits.source: SHARPSHEETS NPI FDD analysis; Vetted Biz
totalUnits.note: Down from ~214 in 2022; 2024 FDD shows ~203 franchised per one analysis.

trainingWeeks.note: Two weeks at the Omaha headquarters plus operational manuals.

businessModelType.source: NPI franchisor site (residential and commercial inspections)

mobileBased.source: Vetted Biz - NPI Franchise Insights (field-based, home-based inspector model)

bankruptcyDisclosed.source: Vetted Biz - NPI FDD analysis (no lawsuits or bankruptcy disclosed)

brands.name: Inspect-It 1st
brands.notes: Property inspection franchise (Inspect-It 1st, Phoenix, AZ; parent Merrymeeting Group), founded 1991 and franchising since 1998. Low-cost, home/mobile-based owner-operator model. Actively recruiting unit franchisees and Regional Directors across the US except AK, HI, RI, ND, and SD - which is captured in statesOpen below. Very small system (~29 units).

riskFlags.title: Very small system after a long operating history
riskFlags.detail: Inspect-It 1st reports roughly 29 operating units despite franchising since 1998 - a small, slow-growth network that limits brand recognition and national marketing leverage relative to larger inspection franchises like Pillar To Post, WIN, and HomeTeam.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Gator Inspect-It 1st directory

riskFlags.title: Revenue tied to cyclical home-sale transaction volume
riskFlags.detail: As with all home-inspection franchises, demand is driven by real-estate transaction volume; rising interest rates and slow housing markets directly compress inspection order flow for a single-operator business with limited diversification.
riskFlags.source: Gaebler Ventures home-inspection franchise overview; category analysis

riskFlags.title: Inconsistent recruitment signals across directories
riskFlags.detail: While the franchisor markets active recruitment of unit owners and Regional Directors, some third-party directories simultaneously show 'not currently accepting inquiries / more opportunities coming soon' - prospective buyers should confirm current availability directly with the franchisor.
riskFlags.source: Franchise.com and Franchise Gator Inspect-It 1st listings

statesOpen.note: Franchisor markets unit-franchise and Regional Director opportunities across the US EXCEPT Alaska, Hawaii, Rhode Island, North Dakota, and South Dakota. Confirm specific territory availability directly with the franchisor.

totalInvestmentMin.note: FDD-derived range $27,400-$38,400; one directory cites $32,500-$43,400 and another $45,000-$56,000.

royaltyPercent.source: Franchise Help / category directories (8% inspection-franchise norm; some sources cite 1st Inspection Services at 8%)
royaltyPercent.note: Royalty not consistently published for Inspect-It 1st specifically; 8% is the cited figure for the affiliated 1st Inspection Services brand. Confirm against current FDD.

yearFounded.source: Franchising.com Inspect-It 1st overview (founded by Richard Schneider)

trainingWeeks.note: Two-week Southwest Institute of Property Inspection (SIPI) program plus 26-week coaching.

businessModelType.source: Inspect-It 1st franchisor listings (residential and commercial inspections)

mobileBased.source: Inspect-It 1st franchisor listings (field-based, home-based inspector model)

fddAvailable.source: Franchise Gator / Vetted Biz Inspect-It 1st directories

brands.name: HomeTeam Inspection Service
brands.notes: Home inspection franchise (HomeTeam Inspection Service, Inc., Milford, OH; incorporated 1992), part of the Authority Brands portfolio since 2020. Known for a multi-inspector 'team' model that completes inspections faster. ~200 US franchises, all franchised. Franchisor does not publish a state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Litigation and/or bankruptcy disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services report that HomeTeam discloses lawsuit and/or bankruptcy information in its Franchise Disclosure Document. Prospective buyers should review the current FDD Items 3 and 4 directly before relying on the disclosure history.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - HomeTeam Inspection Service FDD analysis

riskFlags.title: Revenue tied to cyclical home-sale transaction volume
riskFlags.detail: Inspection demand tracks real-estate transaction volume, so franchisee revenue is exposed to interest-rate-driven housing slowdowns; HomeTeam's average gross sales of about $231,203 leave limited cushion when transaction volume falls.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - HomeTeam Inspection Service Franchise Insights (Item 19-derived)

riskFlags.title: Stacked ongoing fees (6% royalty + 3% ad fund)
riskFlags.detail: Beyond the 6% royalty (collected weekly via EFT), franchisees pay a 3% advertising fund contribution - a combined 9% of sales ongoing burden on a modest-revenue, single-operator inspection business.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - HomeTeam Inspection Service Franchise Insights

brands.name: AmeriSpec Inspection Services
brands.notes: Home inspection franchise (AmeriSpec, Cordova/Memphis, TN), founded 1987 and franchising since 1988, part of the Neighborly portfolio since the 2020 ServiceMaster transaction. Low-cost, home/mobile-based owner-operator model with ~102-137 US/Canada units (all franchised). Franchisor does not publish a state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Low average revenue with high stacked ongoing fees
riskFlags.detail: Item 19-derived average yearly gross sales are about $81,821 against a 7% royalty plus a 3% ad fund (plus a $55/month technology fee and $600-$800 annual software fee) - a high effective fee load on a low-revenue single-operator model, with a 7-9 year estimated payback.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS AmeriSpec FDD analyses

riskFlags.title: Revenue tied to cyclical home-sale transaction volume
riskFlags.detail: Inspection demand tracks real-estate transaction volume, exposing franchisee revenue to interest-rate-driven housing slowdowns; the low average AUV leaves limited cushion in down markets.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS AmeriSpec FDD analysis; category analysis

riskFlags.title: One brand among many in the Neighborly portfolio
riskFlags.detail: AmeriSpec sits inside Neighborly, a large multi-brand franchisor platform that controls development priorities and shared services across dozens of home-service concepts; franchisees are one brand among many in capital-allocation decisions.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS (Neighborly acquired AmeriSpec from ServiceMaster in 2020)

totalInvestmentMin.source: Vetted Biz - AmeriSpec Franchise Insights; SHARPSHEETS

totalInvestmentMax.source: Vetted Biz - AmeriSpec Franchise Insights; SHARPSHEETS

adFundPercent.note: 3% ad fund plus a $55/month technology fee and $600-$800 annual software fee.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Average yearly gross sales; 7-9 year estimated payback.

totalUnits.note: 137 locations across the US and Canada per SHARPSHEETS; Vetted Biz cites 102 US locations. All franchised; 0 company-owned.

brands.name: Woofie's
brands.notes: Mobile pet-care franchise (Woofie's Pet Ventures) offering combined pet sitting, dog walking, and mobile pet-spa/grooming-van services; founded 2004 in Virginia, franchising since 2018, acquired into the Authority Brands portfolio. Fast-growing system (3 to 82 franchised units across 2022-2024) with a strong multi-year revenue ramp. Franchisor does not publish a public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Very young, hyper-growth system with limited mature-unit data
riskFlags.detail: Franchised units grew from 3 (start of 2022) to 9, 39, and 82 by end of 2024 - most of the system is under two years old. The Item 19 also shows extreme dispersion (median territory revenue $124,534 vs an average of $271,604; range $20,303-$3,221,694), so headline averages overstate typical early-stage results.
riskFlags.source: Woofie's 2026 FDD outlet tables and Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Mandatory $28,000/year local marketing spend
riskFlags.detail: Beyond the 6.5% royalty and 2% national brand fund, franchisees must spend $28,000 per calendar year on local marketing (prorated in the first year) - a fixed obligation that pressures Year-1 units still ramping (average Year-1 revenue was just $119,657).
riskFlags.source: Woofie's 2026 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Multi-service model concentrates operational complexity
riskFlags.detail: Woofie's combines pet sitting, dog walking, and mobile grooming vans in one unit - more revenue streams but also more staffing, scheduling, and vehicle/equipment overhead than a single-service pet franchise, raising execution risk for new owners.
riskFlags.source: Woofie's 2026 FDD review (Franchise Chatter); 1851 Franchise

adFundPercent.note: 2% national brand fund, plus a mandatory $28,000/year local marketing spend.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Woofie's 2026 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2024 average revenue per territory across 38 territories (23 franchisees); average per-franchisee revenue $448,737; median territory $124,534; range $20,303-$3,221,694; top 25% averaged $1,061,815.

totalUnits.source: Woofie's 2026 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)
totalUnits.note: 82 franchised + 0 company-owned at end of 2024 (company-owned units converted to franchised by end of 2023).

isGrowing.source: Woofie's 2026 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter)

veteranDiscount.note: Veteran discounts and women-owned franchise support advertised; amount not published.

mobileBased.source: Woofie's franchisor site (mobile pet-spa vans and in-home pet care)

brands.name: Central Bark
brands.notes: Dog daycare, boarding, grooming, training, and retail franchise (Central Bark, founded 2003 in Wisconsin; franchising since 2004). Whole-dog-care facility model with ~41 franchised units, all franchised. Franchisor does not publish a public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: High build-out cost for a facility-based pet model
riskFlags.detail: 2026 FDD Item 7 runs $569,200-$1,394,250 for a full daycare/boarding facility build-out, a substantial capital commitment with an estimated multi-year payback; underperformance is costly given the heavy fixed real-estate and equipment base.
riskFlags.source: Central Bark 2026 FDD (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Wide revenue dispersion and decelerating same-unit growth
riskFlags.detail: 2024 Item 19: average gross sales $825,930 (median $770,229) but a range of $216,972-$1,828,699, with average operating profit of 15.1%. Year-over-year same-store growth decelerated from 24.9% (2022) to 12.2% (2023) to 5.0% (2024).
riskFlags.source: Central Bark 2026 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review)

riskFlags.title: Small system with slow net unit growth
riskFlags.detail: Franchised units grew only modestly (+2, +3, +3) from 35 to 41 across 2022-2024 - steady but slow for a 20-year-old brand, limiting national marketing scale relative to larger pet-care franchisors.
riskFlags.source: Central Bark 2026 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter review)

brands.name: K9 Resorts
brands.notes: Upscale dog daycare and boarding franchise (K-9 Franchising LLC, marketed as K9 Resorts Luxury Pet Hotel), founded 2005 and franchising since 2011. Capital-intensive facility model ($1.5M-$3.6M) with roughly 40 open locations and a large development pipeline (~165 open or in development across 28 states). Franchisor does not publish a public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: Multimillion-dollar facility build with high cash requirement
riskFlags.detail: FDD Item 7 runs roughly $1,481,374-$3,604,802 with a $400,000 minimum cash requirement and an 8-10 year estimated payback. Most deals are multi-unit developer agreements, so the true capital commitment for a typical buyer is well above a single-unit figure.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS K9 Resorts FDD analyses

riskFlags.title: Litigation and/or bankruptcy disclosed in FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services report that K9 Resorts discloses lawsuit and/or bankruptcy information in its Franchise Disclosure Document. Prospective buyers should review the current FDD Items 3 and 4 directly.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - K9 Resorts FDD analysis

riskFlags.title: Large development pipeline relative to open units
riskFlags.detail: K9 Resorts markets ~165 locations 'operating or in various stages of development' across 28 states against only about 40 open units - a pipeline roughly 4x the operating base, heavily dependent on multi-unit developers actually building out their commitments.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; K9 Resorts press (Oct 2024)

liquidCapitalMin.source: Vetted Biz - K9 Resorts Franchise Insights

brands.name: Pet Wants
brands.notes: Fresh pet-food and supplies franchise (Pet Wants, founded 2010; franchising since 2015 under Strategic Franchising Systems) selling proprietary fresh pet food via local delivery, mobile, and small-store/storefront formats. ~159 franchised units, all franchised. Franchisor does not publish a public state-by-state availability list, so state availability is omitted.

riskFlags.title: High royalty plus branding contribution with monthly minimums
riskFlags.detail: Royalty is 7% of gross revenues (minimum $300/month) plus a national branding contribution of the greater of $250/month or 2% of gross revenues - fixed minimums totaling at least $550/month that pressure low-volume territories early on.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz / SHARPSHEETS Pet Wants FDD analyses

riskFlags.title: Dependence on franchisor-supplied proprietary food
riskFlags.detail: The model centers on selling Pet Wants' proprietary fresh pet food, so franchisee economics depend on the franchisor's product formulations, pricing, freshness/shelf-life logistics, and supply chain - factors the franchisee does not control, in a competitive premium pet-food market.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS / Franchise Direct Pet Wants overviews

riskFlags.title: One brand within the Strategic Franchising Systems platform
riskFlags.detail: Pet Wants is part of Strategic Franchising Systems, a multi-brand franchisor; development priorities and shared services are allocated across the portfolio, and franchisees are one concept among several.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz / Franchise Direct Pet Wants overviews
